Title: A shared cis-regulatory module activates transcription in the suspensor of plant embryos
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Scarlet Runner Bean, Common Bean, Tobacco, Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Phaseolus coccineus; Phaseolus vulgaris; Nicotiana tabacum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hammond’s Dwarf Red Flower; G19833; SR1; NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: giant suspensor of early bean embryos
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Accumulates to high levels specifically in the giant suspensor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suspensor-specific transcription in divergent plant embryos via shared cis-regulatory module
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previous promoter deletion/mutagenesis studies; comparative cis-regulatory analysis in this paper
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native G564 upstream cis-regulatory module; comparative context only in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G564 is a suspensor-specific marker gene with a cis-regulatory module composed of 10-bp, Region 2, and Fifth motifs. 2. This paper shows that the same motif types are also required for GA 20-oxidase suspensor transcription. 3. G564 and GA 20-oxidase likely belong to a shared suspensor gene regulatory network.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of suspensor-specific transcription; no direct nutrient-metabolic enzymatic role established
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Its suspensor-specific cis module could be used to drive embryo-region-specific transgene expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as a comparison gene and prior model for suspensor cis-regulatory architecture rather than a nutrient metabolism gene itself.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Candidate MYB TFs; candidate zinc finger TFs

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in both embryo proper and suspensor, elevated in suspensor
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative sequence analysis; DAP-seq-informed motif inference; RNA-seq/EST support
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a strong candidate TF for binding the Region 2 motif. 2. Shows elevated expression in the suspensor. 3. Not functionally validated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putative activator of suspensor transcription through the Region 2 motif; not experimentally proven here
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE57537; DAP-seq database evidence
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only, not a core experimentally validated gene in the Results.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq database match to Region 2-like motif
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis C2H2 zinc finger TF binds a sequence nearly identical to the Region 2 motif. 2. Used here to infer candidate TFs for the suspensor cis module. 3. Not experimentally tested in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background comparative candidate, not a core gene of the study.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GenBank: FJ535441; Common Bean ortholog Phvul.010G087500
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: giant suspensor of early bean embryos
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in globular-stage suspensor; activated in the basal cell of the two-cell embryo and later throughout the suspensor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suspensor-specific reporter expression in globular-stage transgenic tobacco embryos driven by the SRB upstream region
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In situ hybridization; promoter 5′ deletion analysis; gain-of-function reporter assays with CaMV 35S minimal promoter; site-directed mutagenesis of cis motifs; transgenic tobacco GUS assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SRB GA 20-oxidase upstream region; CaMV 35S minimal promoter (constitutive minimal promoter in GOF constructs)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SRB GA 20-oxidase is highly expressed in the giant suspensor of globular-stage embryos. 2. The -341 to +238 region is sufficient to direct suspensor-specific transcription in transgenic tobacco embryos. 3. Two Region 2 motifs, one Fifth motif, and two 10-bp motifs are required for suspensor transcription, demonstrating use of a shared cis-regulatory module with G564.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports suspensor-specific activation of gibberellin biosynthesis gene expression, implying promotion of GA production in the suspensor
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a suspensor-specific regulatory module for embryo-targeted engineering of GA-related traits or other metabolic pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper mainly dissects cis-regulation of this pathway gene rather than enzymatic flux directly; a separate embryo proper module was mapped to -2000 to -1500.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Candidate MYB TFs; candidate C2H2 zinc finger TF related to Phvul.007G253000

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: giant suspensor of early bean embryos
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed specifically within giant suspensors; activated in the basal region after fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In situ hybridization / prior expression analysis referenced in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA 3-oxidase mRNA localizes to the giant suspensor. 2. Along with GA 20-oxidase, it is activated in the basal region after fertilization. 3. Its expression supports the conclusion that the full GA biosynthetic pathway is active in the suspensor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Implies local production of bioactive gibberellins in the suspensor
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as part of the coexpressed GA pathway, but not functionally dissected as the primary subject of promoter analysis in Results.
